_meta.purpose: Sanitized executive-report priors for the Tessera demo. Distilled from the AccessQuint vCISO practice. NO raw client spreadsheet rows are present; this is the only artifact that goes public.

findings.title: Vendor Risk — 8 open findings

items.text: 3 critical vendors have no current SOC 2 Type II report on file.
Vendor register not reconciled against active data-sharing agreements.
Re-assessment cadence undefined for 5 medium-risk vendors.
DPA addenda missing for 3 low-risk SaaS tools.

steps: Collect current SOC 2 Type II reports for the 3 critical vendors (30-day SLA).
Reconcile the vendor register against signed data-sharing agreements.
Define and calendar a risk-tiered re-assessment cadence.

findings.title: Access Control — 7 open findings

items.text: 23 accounts authenticated without MFA in the audit window; 3 are admin roles.
Privileged access not reviewed in the last 90 days.
2 service accounts share a static, non-rotated credential.
Joiner/mover/leaver tickets missing access-revocation evidence (3 of 14).
MFA not enforced to phishing-resistant factors.

steps: Enforce conditional-access MFA on all admin roles — remediate the 3 flagged admins within 7 days.
Run a privileged-access review and capture sign-off as evidence.
Re-run CC6.1 evidence collection to confirm closure and extend the Merkle chain.

splunk.label: View the sealed CC6.1 verdict & evidence chain →

findings.title: Incident Response — 7 open findings

items.text: No tested runbook for credential-compromise; tabletop overdue >12 months.
Mean time-to-contain (MTTC) is not measured.
Post-incident reviews not consistently documented.
On-call contact tree is out of date.

steps: Adopt and tabletop a credential-compromise runbook this quarter.
Instrument MTTC/MTTR and report monthly.
Standardize a post-incident review template.

findings.title: Logging & Monitoring — 2 open findings

items.text: Authentication failures and MFA-less logins are not alerted in near-real-time.
Log retention below the 1-year policy for one source.

steps: Create a detection for repeated failed / MFA-less authentications.
Extend retention on the affected source to meet the 1-year policy.

splunk.label: Open the live Compliance Posture data →

prep._source: distilled from GRC trackers (aggregates only — no raw client rows)
prep.vq: Who can access what, how is access provisioned/reviewed, and is MFA enforced?

maturity.tag: Risk trending down across KRIs · two-window drift

findings.title: Vendor Risk — 8 open findings

items.text: 2 vendors that were compliant in window 1 lapsed their SOC 2 by window 2.
New sub-processor added between windows without a risk review.
3 low-risk DPAs still pending signature across both windows.

steps: Re-collect lapsed vendor attestations before the next assessment.
Gate new sub-processors behind a risk review.

findings.title: Access Control — 7 open findings

items.text: Passed in window 1 (Q1, 1,247 events) — 23 non-MFA auths reappeared in window 2 (Q2, 1,809). Silent regression.
A conditional-access policy was disabled between windows.
Drift not caught because re-assessment cadence is annual, not continuous.

steps: Re-enable the disabled conditional-access policy.
Move CC7.2 monitoring from annual to scheduled (continuous) evaluation.
Alert on window-over-window verdict regressions.

splunk.label: Compare the two windows in Splunk →

findings.title: Incident Response — 7 open findings

items.text: Detection coverage that existed in window 1 was not present in window 2.
Escalation SLA slipped between windows.

steps: Restore the missing detection content.
Re-baseline the escalation SLA and monitor for drift.

findings.title: Logging & Monitoring — 2 open findings

items.text: A log source feeding window 1 stopped ingesting before window 2.
Retention reduced on one index between windows.

steps: Restore the dropped log source and backfill if possible.
Add a data-ingest health monitor.

splunk.label: Open the live Compliance Posture data →

drift._source: distilled from GRC trackers (aggregates only — no raw client rows)
drift.vq: Did a control that passed earlier quietly regress between assessments?

trust.vq: Can a board trust AI-generated evidence — and prove it wasn't altered?

findings.title: Every verdict cross-checked by four independent vendors
findings.rem: Maintain the four-vendor panel; single-vendor fallback is logged and flagged.

items.text: 4 of 4 vendors empaneled for every verdict in the window.
Single-vendor fallback events: 0 (each would be logged and flagged).

steps: Maintain the four-vendor panel; alert on any single-vendor fallback.

splunk.label: View the cross-checked verdicts in Splunk →

findings.title: Tampering is provable — any edit breaks the SHA-256 chain
findings.rem: Publish the chain root with each report; auditors re-verify at /verify.

items.text: 6 of 6 snapshots verified intact on the last check.
Chain root is published with each report; a 1-byte edit breaks verification.

steps: Publish the chain root with every report; auditors re-verify at /verify.

trust._source: curated — not yet in GRC sheets

aigov.vq: Is there proof — not just policy — that AI systems are governed and monitored?

findings.title: Model inventory exists on paper but is not evidenced in logs
findings.rem: Instrument model/agent registration to Splunk; reconcile inventory weekly.

items.text: 12 models in the registry; 4 have no corresponding runtime log evidence.
No automated reconciliation between the registry and deployed models.

steps: Instrument model/agent registration to Splunk.
Reconcile the inventory against runtime logs weekly.

findings.title: Agent decisions not consistently logged (OWASP Agentic Top 10)
findings.refs: ISO 42001 A.6.2.2 · NIST AI RMF MEASURE
findings.rem: Capture agent-decision traces to a tamper-evident index.

items.text: Agent-decision traces captured for only 64% of runs.
No tamper-evident index dedicated to agent decisions.

steps: Capture agent-decision traces to a tamper-evident index.
Set a coverage SLO (≥ 95%) and alert below it.

aigov._source: curated — not yet in GRC sheets
